text:
There was once a farmer named Harold who had a large and beautiful garden. The garden was full of bright flowers and delicious fruits that Harold tended with great care.

One sunny day, a traveling salesman came to Harold's village. He carried a map that pointed towards mysterious lands full of strange fruits that smelled heavenly. Seeing the potential opportunity to enrich his harvest, Harold became curious about these unknown fruits.

That evening, after a long day, Harold sat beside his lovely wife, Elizabeth. They talked about the mysterious fruits, dreaming of the wonderful tastes they could enjoy.

Inspired, Harold decided to venture into that strange land. His journey was perilous; he had to cross a vast desert that smelled weird, navigate through thick woods filled with odd birds chirping strange songs, and climb a mountain that seemed endless.

At last, Harold stumbled upon a strange village. In the center stood a magnificent tree that produced these enchanting fruits. The villagers were welcoming.
